topic.name: Suspension of Tariffs
topic.description: The documents discuss the temporary suspension of duties on various goods in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on certain synthetic staple fibers
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on chocolate crumb
imported in bulk quantities
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on p-Amino Benzoic Acid
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 4: suspend temporarily the duty on basketballs
external surface other than leather or rubber
Document 5: extend the temporary suspension of duty on vinylidene chloride-methyl methacrylate-acrylonitrile copolymer
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 6: extend the temporary suspension of duty on Sulfur Black 1
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 7: extend the temporary suspension of duty on handheld electric can openers
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: Small Business and Housing Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at supporting small businesses and improving housing conditions in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Investment Act of 1958 to increase the amount that a state or local governmental entity may invest in a small business investment company
Requires the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to approve within 60 days after receipt an application for licensing as an SBIC
Document 2: Prohibits the use of an increase in the guarantee fee required to be charged by the Federal National Mortgage Association to offset an increase in outlays
Prohibits the Department of the Treasury from selling, transferring, relinquishing, liquidating, divesting, or otherwise disposing of any outstanding shares of senior preferred stock
Document 3: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require certain mortgage originators or lenders to include a borrower's ability to repay
Instructs the Secretary of the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to make grants to state governments and tribal organizations to assist programs established for foreclosure mitigation
Document 4: Directs the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish a program for encore entrepreneurs
Defines 'encore entrepreneur' to mean an entrepreneur who is seeking to start a new small business concern
Document 5: Amends the Small Business Act with respect to the procurement program for women-owned small businesses
Allow a contracting officer to award a sole source contract to any economically disadvantaged women-owned small business
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to carry out a pilot program to use authority under the National Housing Act
Requires the Secretary to make payments to a qualified entity to compensate for their costs of making in-person contact with mortgagors
Document 7: Amends the National Housing Act with respect to mortgage insurance eligibility
Increases high-cost area and project-based additional mortgage loan limits for FHA-insured mortgages

topic.name: Telecommunications Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on regulating telecommunications services, privacy, and security measures in the context of electronic communications.

Document 1: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to direct the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to adopt a final rule establishing support for broadband under the Universal Service Fund Lifeline Assistance Program
Requires the program to be technology neutral to promote competition from service providers.
Document 4: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to expand the prohibition on the provision of inaccurate caller identification information
Revises the definitions of 'caller identification information' and 'caller identification service' to include text messages.

topic.name: Educational Accountability and Improvement
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing student academic achievement, accountability systems, and educational opportunities for diverse student populations.

Document 7: Amends the school improvement program to require states to include information on military-connected students in their annual state report cards.
Requires states to report how military-connected students performed in comparison to all their other students.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code that aim to modify tax regulations and benefits for individuals and organizations.

Document 1: excludes from gross income payments received by a student under a comprehensive student work-learning-service program
operated by a work college.
Document 2: prohibits denial of a federal tax exemption under the Internal Revenue Code to any church, synagogue, mosque, temple, or other house of worship
because it engages in activity protected by the U.S. Constitution.
Document 3: expands the eligibility of certain small insurance companies for the alternative corporate income tax
by increasing the premium limitation used to determine such eligibility.
Document 4: allows a permanent 100% exclusion from gross income of gain from the sale or exchange of qualified small business stock
repeal the 28% capital gains tax rate on qualified small business stock.
Document 5: allows individual taxpayers an exclusion from gross income for certain items of partnership and S corporation pass-thru income
up to $250,000 ($500,000 for married couples filing joint returns).
Document 6: increases the amount of wages which may be taken into account in determining the allowable work opportunity tax credit
for the hiring of certain disabled veterans.
Document 7: allows a taxpayer who uses a residence to conduct a trade or business a standard tax deduction
equal to the lesser of $1,500 or the taxpayer's gross trade or business income.

topic.name: Water and Environmental Management
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative measures aimed at improving water management, environmental conservation, and related scientific research in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Interior... to help maintain and restore the estuary while enhancing the quality and reliability of California's water supply.
...to commission additional studies by NAS to provide more strategic advice on water and environmental management challenges facing the Delta.
Document 2: ...authorize appropriations for FY2011-FY2014 to the Secretary of the Interior to carry out certain activities and services related to fish and wildlife...
...requires the Secretary to submit a report to Congress... evaluating the accomplishments of the volunteer program, the community partnerships program, and the refuge education programs.
Document 3: ...requires the Administrator of each of the Bonneville Power Administration... to include in monthly billing statements estimates of each customer's share of such Administration's direct and indirect costs for compliance with the Endangered Species Act of 1973.
...submit an annual report estimating such costs to the House Committee on Natural Resources and the Senate Committee on Environment and Public Works.
Document 4: ...establishes a national forensic science research program to improve, expand, and coordinate federal research in forensic sciences.

...requires the Director of the National Science Foundation to enter into a contract with the National Academy of Sciences to develop a report identifying the most critical forensic science disciplines that require further research.
Document 5: ...requires NOAA... to direct research and take action to improve oil spill prevention, response, and recovery in Arctic waters.
...requires the Coast Guard to assess and take action to reduce the risk of, and improve U.S. response to, a maritime disaster in the Beaufort and Chukchi Seas.
Document 6: ...requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency to establish the Water Infrastructure Resiliency and Sustainability Program to provide grants to owners or operators of water systems.
...gives priority to owners or operators of water systems that are at the greatest and most immediate risk of facing significant negative impacts due to changing hydrologic conditions.
Document 7: ...expresses the sense of Congress that water and sanitation are critically important resources that impact many aspects of human life.
...directs the Administrator of USAID to oversee water, sanitation, and hygiene programs.

topic.name: Renewable Energy Legislation
topic.description: A collection of legislative acts focused on promoting and regulating renewable energy sources and alternative fuels in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the Renewable Fuel Program
defines 'domestic alternative fuel' as ethanol that is produced from domestic fossil fuels
Document 2: modifies the tax credit for producing electricity from closed or open-loop biomass facilities
extend the credit period for such production
Document 3: amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase tax credits for solar property
applies to solar property with a nameplate capacity of less than 20 kilowatts
Document 4: redefines the term 'cellulosic biomass ethanol'
increases the volume of renewable fuel that gasoline is required to contain
Document 5: allows an Indian tribe to assign production from a renewable energy resource facility
for purposes of the renewable electricity production tax credit
Document 6: allows an income-based refundable tax credit for residential energy costs
defines 'residential energy costs' as amounts paid to any utility for electricity or natural gas
Document 7: modifies the tax credit for alternative fuel vehicle refueling property
increases to 75% the rate of such credit for property using 85% ethanol fuel

topic.name: Missing Persons and Child Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative measures aimed at addressing issues related to missing persons, child protection, and the management of sex offenders.

Document 1: authorizes the Department of Justice to maintain public databases of missing persons
requires law enforcement agencies to submit missing child reports to NamUs
Document 2: authorizes appropriations for the Sex Offender Management Assistance program
requires states to locate and apprehend sex offenders
Document 3: requires the Attorney General to allow community organizations to distribute sexual barrier protection devices in federal correctional facilities
develop and implement a strategy to reduce STIs in such facilities
Document 4: directs the Attorney General to investigate violations of civil rights statutes resulting in death
authorizes grants for investigation and prosecution of civil rights violations
Document 5: reauthorizes the Missing Alzheimer's Disease Patient Alert Program
provides competitive grants to locate missing patients with Alzheimer's disease
Document 6: requires courts to impose additional assessments on persons convicted of specified offenses
establishes a national child abuse hotline and public education on child abuse
Document 7: requires state plans for foster care to assist alien children in achieving lawful permanent resident status
authorizes grants to assist in achieving special immigrant juvenile status

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the provision and management of benefits and support services for veterans, including legal assistance, healthcare, and financial aid.

Document 1: Amends the Justice for All Act of 2004 to extend funding for the grant program to provide legal assistance to victims of crime.
Document 2: Exempts veterans from copayment for opioid antagonists at high risk for overdose.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to pay an interim benefit of $500 per month for delayed claims.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of the Army to implement the First Sergeants Barracks Initiative throughout the Army.
Document 5: Prohibits the Secretary of Veterans Affairs from collecting debts from veterans who die as a result of service-connected disabilities.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to furnish headstones for veterans buried in private cemeteries.
Document 7: Amends provisions authorizing educational loan repayment for health professionals serving in the armed forces.

topic.name: Military Justice Reform
topic.description: Legislative efforts aimed at improving the military justice system and addressing issues related to courts-martial and the treatment of service members accused of offenses.

Document 1: Amends the Uniform Code of Military Justice (UCMJ) to direct the Secretaries of Defense (DOD) and Homeland Security (DHS) to require the Secretaries of the military departments to modify the process for determining whether to try by court-martial a member accused of certain UCMJ offenses.

Requires courts-martial determinations for such offenses to be made by a commissioned officer available for detail as trial counsel who is outside the chain of command of the member subject to the charges.
Document 2: Amends the Uniform Code of Military Justice (UCMJ) to direct the Secretaries of Defense (DOD) and Homeland Security (DHS) to require the Secretaries of the military departments to modify the process for determining whether to try by court-martial a member accused of certain UCMJ offenses.

Requires courts-martial determinations for such offenses to be made by a commissioned officer available for detail as trial counsel who is outside the chain of command of the member subject to the charges.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of the military department concerned to suspend all or a portion of the pay and allowances of a member of the Armed Forces who is being held in confinement pending trial by court-martial or by civil authority for any sex-related or capital offense.
Entitles such member to written notice of the suspension and representation by an attorney or other representative in making such request.

topic.name: Regulatory Measures in Telecommunications and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative actions and regulatory measures aimed at enhancing telecommunications, internet access, and safety standards across various sectors.

Document 1: Amends the Ethics in Government Act of 1978 to impose a fine and/or prison term for intentional failure to file financial information.
Document 2: Restores rules adopted by the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) regarding the open Internet and broadband industry practices.
Document 3: Amends the Telecommunications Act of 1996 to declare FCC's authority regarding state laws on broadband Internet access.
Document 4: Amends the federal criminal code to make it a crime to interfere with radio frequencies used by emergency responders.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to ensure covered motor carrier data is not publicly available until certain recommendations are implemented.
Document 6: Directs the FAA to issue regulations for continuous tracking of aircraft information during flight.
Document 7: Requires retail establishments to notify customers about the use of mobile device tracking technology.

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Conservation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts related to the management, conservation, and restoration of federal lands and resources in the United States.

Document 1: authorizes stewardship contracting projects to achieve land management goals
defines eligible land as National Forest System land or BLM land
Document 2: directs the Secretary of the Interior to develop a multipurpose cadastre of federal real property
assists with federal land management and resource conservation
Document 3: directs the Secretary of Agriculture to conserve and restore forest and watershed health
establishes the Eastside Forest Scientific and Technical Advisory Panel
Document 4: requires land to be held in trust for the Duckwater Shoshone Tribe
directs the Secretary of the Interior to complete a survey of boundary lines
Document 5: designates federal lands in Monongahela National Forest as wilderness
requires development of a plan for enhanced nonmotorized recreation trail opportunities
Document 6: authorizes appropriations for fish recovery programs in the Upper Colorado River
provides for the annual base funding for recovery implementation programs
Document 7: includes Juab County for funding feasibility studies related to groundwater management
development of systems for conjunctive use of surface and groundwater resources

topic.name: Intelligence and National Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislation related to intelligence activities, surveillance, and regulatory frameworks governing national security operations.

Document 1: authorizes FY2017 appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
the Office of the Director of National Intelligence (ODNI); the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA); the Department of Defense (DOD)
Document 3: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to establish a new process
the FBI submits an application to a FISA court for an order requiring the production of business records
Document 4: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to add a new title concerning additional procedures for acquiring the communications of certain persons outside the United States

authorizes the Attorney General (AG) and Director of National Intelligence (DNI) to jointly authorize, for periods up to one year, the targeting (electronic surveillance) of persons located outside the United States
Document 5: amends the Federal Information Security Management Act of 2002 (FISMA) to reestablish the oversight authority of the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB)
ensuring complementary and uniform standards for information systems and national security systems
Document 7: amends the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) to require federal agencies to make information that is disclosable under such Act available to the public
establish a presumption of openness in administering FOIA by prohibiting an agency from withholding information otherwise disclosable under FOIA

topic.name: Water Resource Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and initiatives aimed at the management, conservation, and quality of water resources in rural and urban settings.

Document 1: Establishes the Reclamation Rural Water Construction and Settlement Implementation Fund
Permits the Department of the Interior to use specified amounts available in the Rural Water Project Account, for each of FY2015-FY2035, to complete construction of rural water projects
Document 2: Requires the Department of the Interior to implement the Agreement between the United States and Westlands Water District
Each San Luis Unit Contractor shall assume all legal responsibility for the management of drainage water within, and shall not discharge drain water outside of, its boundaries
Document 3: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to reauthorize the Chesapeake Bay Program
Authorizes the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to provide technical assistance and assistance grants to soil conservation districts
Document 4: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to include among eligible grant activities the development and implementation of programs for source tracking, sanitary surveys, and prevention efforts to address the identified sources of beach water pollution
Requires grant recipients to identify rapid testing methods and measures for communication within 24 hours of the results of a water sample concerning pollutants
Document 5: Requires the release of water from the marketable yield pool of water stored in the Ruedi Reservoir for the benefit of endangered fish habitat
Provides that the release shall satisfy 50% of the commitment by certain water users to provide water per year
Document 6: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to redefine 'navigable waters'
Prohibits activities carried out by the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from impinging upon states' power over land and water use
Document 7: Requires the head of a federal agency to ensure that the system intake is protected by a covering sufficient to ensure that a child may not be swept into the system
Requires compliance with this Act as a condition for the award of federal assistance for projects.

topic.name: Communications and Environmental Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures related to communications infrastructure, public safety, and environmental policies.

Document 1: reauthorize and revise provisions expiring under the Satellite Television Extension and Localism Act of 2010
prohibit multichannel video programming distributors from retransmitting broadcasting station signals without the authority of the originating station
Document 2: establish a federal greenhouse gas (GHG) registry
requires each affected facility to submit periodic reports to the Administrator for inclusion in the Registry
Document 3: provisions concerning the reduction of global warming pollution
require the establishment of both national transportation related GHG emissions reduction goals
Document 4: requires a satellite carrier whose secondary transmissions are subject to statutory licensing to deposit a filing fee semiannually
extends through December 31, 2014 the earliest date through which voluntary royalty agreements remain in effect
Document 5: increase the electromagnetic spectrum allocation for public safety services
establish rules to permit a public safety broadband licensee to authorize public safety service providers
Document 6: conduct a competition to award grants to eligible entities for the development of nonstationary radio over Internet protocol (RoIP) devices
requires the Assistant Secretary to establish requirements for the RoIP devices developed
Document 7: authorizes TransCanada Keystone Pipeline, L.P. to construct, connect, operate, and maintain the pipeline
grants original and exclusive jurisdiction to the U.S. Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit over any civil action for the review of a federal agency action regarding the pipeline

topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Appropriations
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative amendments and appropriations related to federal programs and regulations in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the National Institute of Standards and Technology Act
directs the Secretary to report to Congress on such cost share requirements
Document 2: Directs the Attorney General to make grants to assist states
awards bonus payments to states that have implemented and used an enhanced DNA collection process
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain ice cream makers
Document 4: Amends the Office of National Drug Control Policy Reauthorization Act of 2006
authorize appropriations for the U.S. Anti-Doping Agency for FY2014-FY2020
Document 5: Allows railroad employees to remain or go on duty for a period in excess of the limitations established under hours-of-service requirements
Document 6: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain tamper resistant ground fault circuit interrupters
Document 7: Extends agricultural programs under the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002
suspends permanent price support authorities through May 23, 2008

topic.name: Social Security and Medicare Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislative acts aimed at improving, modernizing, and protecting benefits under the Social Security and Medicare programs.

Document 1: amends title II of the Social Security Act
increase from $255 to $1,000 the lump-sum death payment
Document 2: Amends title XXI of the Social Security Act
allow qualifying states to use allotments under SCHIP for certain Medicaid expenditures
Document 3: Amends the Congressional Budget Act of 1974
reduces or has the effect of reducing any current benefits under the OASDI programs
Document 4: reduces from age 57 to age 55 the age after which remarriage shall not result in termination of benefits
permits eligibility for such benefits by an individual who remarried before the date of enactment
Document 5: Amends title XI of the Social Security Act
increase civil money penalties for fraud and abuse under the Medicare program
Document 6: Amends title XVIII of the Social Security Act
apply eligibility requirements of Medicare special enrollment periods considering family members' employment status
Document 7: Amends title XVIII of the Social Security Act
waive the monthly part B premium for certain military retirees and their spouses

topic.name: Education and Workforce Development
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing education and training programs to prepare individuals for careers in various industries, including healthcare and green jobs.

Document 1: amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to assist qualified individuals in receiving the education and training needed to become a physician assistant
provides educational institutions with the resources to keep up with the demand for new physician assistants.
Document 2: establish a Green Job Corps program that provides for the development of academic skills and paid jobs in green-collar industries
award YouthBuild Build Green Grants and Green-Collar Youth Opportunity Grants to eligible entities and organizations to provide eligible youth with education, jobs, and training.
Document 3: authorize the Secretary of Commerce to make block grants to a state or local government, an institution of higher education, or a nonprofit economic development organization
promote cluster development programs in such regions and support the staff who operate such programs.
Document 4: requires that any job for which a trainee is hired be a new job
defines 'qualified training' as education or training to provide an individual with the education or skills necessary to perform the job.
Document 5: award competitive grants to institutions of higher education for educational or career training programs
provide job training or recruiting activities necessary to provide skilled workers for businesses.
Document 6: award competitive grants to states and local educational agencies for the development of environmental justice curricula
link students with career opportunities in the environmental field.
Document 7: requires the Foundation Board to appoint a chief operating officer for the Foundation
authorizes appropriations for FY2008-FY2010.

topic.name: Education Policy and Oversight
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving education access, oversight, and quality for various educational institutions and demographics.

Document 1: establish an Office of Rural Education Policy
provide technical assistance and other support for rural education improvement efforts
Document 2: increase the number of students from low-income families who attend college
implement a comprehensive college guidance program
Document 4: coordinate federal oversight of proprietary institutions of higher education
protect students from unfair, deceptive, abusive, unethical, fraudulent, or predatory practices
Document 5: authorize appropriations to the National Science Foundation for education and human resources
support undergraduate education
Document 6: authorize appropriations for the National Science Foundation
broaden secondary school students' access to, and interest in, careers that require academic preparation in STEM subjects
Document 7: coordinate federal oversight of proprietary institutions of higher education
submit an annual report to Congress that includes recommendations for legislative and administrative actions

topic.name: Offshore Energy Development
topic.description: The documents focus on legislation related to the exploration, leasing, and management of energy resources, particularly oil and gas, in offshore areas and federal lands.

Document 1: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act (OCSLA) to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to implement any agreement for the management of transboundary hydrocarbon reservoirs.

Authorizes the Secretary to implement the terms of the Agreement between the United States of America and the United Mexican States Concerning Transboundary Hydrocarbon Reservoirs in the Gulf of Mexico.
Document 2: Instructs the Secretary of the Interior to establish a cost recovery fee for applications for a permit to drill for oil and gas on federal lands.
Amends the Mineral Leasing Act to extend from 30 days to 90 days the deadline by which the Secretary must either issue or defer a decision on a drilling permit application.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to designate specified federal land in Nevada under the administrative jurisdiction of BLM that is identified as a solar development zone as a solar pilot project area.
Conduct lease sales and issue leases for commercial solar energy development on such land.
Document 4: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act (OCSLA) to direct the Secretary of the Interior to make available for leasing and to conduct lease sales.
Directs the Secretary, in the 2012-2017 5-year oil and gas leasing program, to make available for leasing OCS planning areas that are estimated to contain more than 2.5 billion barrels of oil.
Document 5: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to direct the Secretary of the Interior to make available for leasing and to conduct lease sales including: at least 50% of the available unleased acreage within each outer Continental Shelf (OCS) planning area.
Directs the Secretary, in developing a 5-year oil and gas leasing program, to determine a domestic strategic production goal for the development of oil and natural gas.
Document 6: This bill amends the Geothermal Steam Act of 1970 to categorically exclude from the requirements for an environmental assessment or an environmental impact statement under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 a geothermal exploration test project.
A leaseholder of a geothermal lease on federal land intending to carry out a geothermal exploration test project must provide notice to the Department of the Interior.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of the Interior, in conducting lease sales under the Mineral Leasing Act, to offer for sale at least 25% of the annual nominated acreage not previously made available for lease.
Instructs the Secretary to make nominated areas available for lease within 18 months after an area is designated as open under a current land use plan.

topic.name: Wildlife and Food Safety Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at regulating wildlife protection, food safety, and consumer rights regarding genetically engineered products.

Document 1: Prohibits any person from offering billfish or billfish products for sale
Subjects a person to a maximum civil penalty of $100,000 for each violation.
Document 2: Sets forth a regulatory process for preventing the introduction and establishment in the United States of certain injurious wildlife
Establishes civil and criminal penalties for violations.
Document 3: Deems misbranded any food that has been genetically engineered or contains one or more genetically engineered ingredients
Defines 'genetically engineered' as a material intended for human consumption.
Document 4: Requires each regulated retail seller of any scheduled listed chemical product at retail to submit a self-certification of compliance
Imposes civil penalties for negligent failure to self-certify as required by this Act.
Document 5: Prohibits the manufacture, sale, or distribution in commerce of any children's product containing specified levels of toxic metals
Directs the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) to establish a measurement standard.
Document 6: Authorizes the Department of Agriculture (USDA) to issue a mandatory recall of contaminated meat, poultry, and egg products
Establishes procedures, including civil and criminal penalties, to enforce this bill.
Document 7: Amends the Horse Protection Act to replace the Designated Qualified Persons program
Requires the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to establish a single Horse Industry Organization (HIO).

topic.name: U.S. Immigration and National Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures related to immigration policies, national security, and the legal framework governing the treatment of individuals in relation to terrorism and domestic violence.

Document 1: creates an E-4 treaty trader visa category for up to 15,000 nationals of the Republic of Korea
coming to the United States solely to perform specialty occupation services
Document 2: replaces the term 'unprivileged enemy combatant' with 'unlawful enemy combatant'
allows the President to authorize the detention of any unlawful enemy combatant until determining that the individual is no longer a threat
Document 3: directs a state to provide by law enhanced sentencing requirements for persons convicted of committing domestic violence
prohibits a state that fails within two years to implement this Act from receiving 20% of the funds
Document 4: prohibits the CIA from operating or controlling armed unmanned aerial vehicles
requires the DOD to report to Congress on civilian protection mechanisms
Document 5: expresses the sense of Congress that the Muslim Brotherhood has met the criteria for designation as a foreign terrorist organization
requires the State Department to report to Congress within 60 days
Document 6: prohibits the use of federal funds for the position of Public Advocate within U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement
any other position within ICE whose functions are substantially the same
Document 7: directs the Department of the Treasury to refund expenses incurred by the Department of Homeland Security
maintenance and updating of systems used by the division to identify and track criminal aliens

topic.name: Higher Education Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing educational support and financial aid for students in higher education, including veterans and student-athletes.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to make educational counseling available to students pursuing an approved program of education while using educational assistance.
Requires a student who is a veteran to receive such counseling, unless the student specifically declines.
Document 2: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require an institution of higher education that has an athletic program to comply with specific requirements for student athletes.

A student athlete who becomes medically ineligible shall receive institutional student aid equivalent to the aid the student athlete would have received if he or she had not suffered such injury or illness.
Document 3: Requires institutions of higher education to provide each admitted student considering an undergraduate or graduate program a multi-year tuition and fee schedule.
Directs the Secretary of Education to waive the application of these requirements to IHEs for which the requirements are not practicable.
Document 4: Requires a lender to obtain the IHE's certification of the student's enrollment status, cost of attendance, and estimated financial assistance before issuing a private education loan.
Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require IHEs to determine whether students have exhausted their options for title IV assistance.
Document 5: Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to allow certain borrowers to consolidate their private education loans as Direct Consolidation Loans.
Directs the Secretary of Education to inform and educate students and their families about this loan consolidation program.
Document 6: Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require institutions of higher education to refund to students the tuition and fees paid for education they will not receive due to their withdrawal from school to serve in the Armed Forces.
Provides loan forgiveness under the Federal Family Education Loan, Direct Loan, and Perkins Loan programs to students whose withdrawal from school is necessitated by service in the Armed Forces.
Document 7: Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to allow the Secretary of Education to award a student two Pell Grants during a single award year.

Treats a Teacher Education Assistance for College and Higher Education Grant as a Direct Unsubsidized Stafford Loan only in proportion to the extent to which its recipient fails to comply with the Grant's service obligation.

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Conservation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, conservation, and enhancement of federal lands and natural resources in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the National Trails System Act to add certain existing hiking trails along Lake Superior's north shore
North Country National Scenic Trail in northeastern Minnesota.
Document 2: Amends the National Wildlife Refuge System Administration Act of 1966
designate a portion of the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge as a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Document 3: extends the authority of the Department of the Interior
to carry out the Federal Lands Recreation Enhancement Act.
Document 5: if, by 45 days after a state Bureau of Land Management office has submitted a Federal Register notice
the notice shall consider to be approved.
Document 7: authorize appropriations for FY2012-FY2017 for allocations to states for Indian reservation roads
Indian School Bus Route Safety Reauthorization Act of 2011.

topic.name: Energy Policy and Environmental Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to energy production, particularly fossil fuels, and the associated environmental regulations aimed at managing emissions and pollution.

Document 1: Amends the Energy Independence and Security Act of 2007 to repeal the prohibition against any federal agency contract for procurement of an alternative or synthetic fuel.
Lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions associated with the production and combustion of the fuel must be less than or equal to greenhouse gas emissions from conventional petroleum sources.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase the percentage depletion allowance for domestic crude oil and natural gas production from marginal properties.
Require the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish cost-effective minimal requirements for the prevention of oil and hazardous substance discharges.
Document 3: Amends the Natural Gas Act, the Natural Gas Policy Act of 1978, and the Federal Power Act to authorize the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to issue a temporary cease and desist order.
Grants FERC emergency authority to change or suspend temporarily the rates, terms, or conditions of service.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to provide that no permit shall be required by the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) for certain discharges from recreational vessels.
Directs the Administrator to develop management practices for recreational vessels to mitigate the adverse impacts of such discharges.
Document 5: Amends the Clean Air Act to modify provisions regarding geographical areas that do not attain the national ambient air quality standards (NAAQS) for ozone and carbon monoxide.
Directs the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to require each state to revise the boundaries of nonattainment areas.
Document 6: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to prohibit the Secretary of the Interior from issuing a permit for exploration for or production of oil and gas unless an oil spill response plan has been approved.
Requires regulations to require owners or operators of offshore facilities to have their plans for responding to a worst case discharge approved.
Document 7: Requires the Bureau of Ocean Energy Management (BOEM) to conduct one oil and gas lease sale off North Carolina's coast every year for five years.
Allows coastal states to receive revenue from offshore energy projects.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts related to immigration policies and procedures in the United States.

Document 1: Development, Relief, and Education for Alien Minors Act of 2011 or DREAM Act of 2011
Authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to cancel the removal of, and adjust to the status of an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence on a conditional basis.
Document 2: Haitian Emergency Life Protection Act of 2010 or the H.E.L.P. Act
Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act with respect to V- visa nonimmigrants.
Document 3: Refugee Protection Act of 2016
This bill amends the Immigration and Naturalization Act to eliminate the one-year time limit for filing an asylum claim.
Document 4: Jobs Originated through Launching Travel Act of 2012 or the JOLT Act of 2012
Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to direct the Secretary of State to establish a pilot fee-based premium processing service.
Document 5: Travel Facilitation and Safety Act of 2015
This bill authorizes the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to enter into cost-sharing agreements with airport authorities.
Document 6: Alien Smuggling and Terrorism Prevention Act of 2007
Directs the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to check against all available terrorist watchlists those alien smugglers and smuggled individuals.
Document 7: H-2A Improvement Act
Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include within the H-2A nonimmigrant visa category temporary agricultural workers.

topic.name: Employee Benefits and Protections
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing employee benefits, protections, and rights in various employment contexts.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to modify the definition of 'applicable large employer' for health care coverage mandates.
Excludes from such definition a small employer that is eligible to purchase a qualified health plan.
Document 2: Amends Internal Revenue Code pension plan provisions to apply a special rule for determining the status of an employer participating in a church plan.
Includes within the definition of plan under ERISA an employee benefit plan which is a church plan.
Document 3: Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act to cover employees at worksites that employ fewer than 50 employees.
An employee covered by FMLA may take up to 4 hours during any 30-day period for parental involvement leave.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Labor to review forms for obtaining workers' compensation benefits to reduce paperwork.
Determines whether it is feasible to combine or reduce such forms.
Document 5: Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act to entitle an eligible employee to up to 12 workweeks of leave due to the death of a son or daughter.
Allows such an employee to substitute any available paid leave for any leave without pay.
Document 6: Amends title II of the Social Security Act to revise the formula for computation of the primary insurance amount.
Extends a child's benefit for certain full-time post-secondary students under age 22.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to require certain employers to make available a payroll deposit individual retirement account.
Imposes a penalty on employers who fail to provide eligible employees access to automatic IRA arrangements.

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Military Engagement
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts related to U.S. foreign policy, military engagement, and counter-terrorism efforts.

Document 1: Designates Montenegro and Bosnia and Herzegovina as eligible to receive assistance under the NATO Participation Act
Directs the President to establish and update bilateral programs to assist Bosnia and Herzegovina, Georgia, the Republic of Macedonia, and Montenegro achieve full NATO membership.
Document 2: Directs the Departments of State, Defense, Treasury, and the Director of National Intelligence to submit a 10-year strategy to counter Iranian activities and threats.

The President shall impose against Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) sanctions with respect to blocking property of, and prohibiting transactions with, foreign persons who commit or support terrorism.
Document 3: Directs the President to prohibit all transactions in property and property interests of a person if such property and property interests are in the United States
Describes a 'person' as the state and the government of Iran, or any political subdivision, agency, or instrumentality of such government.
Document 4: The Authorization for Use of Military Force Against Iraq Resolution of 2002 is the sole basis of authority under which the President launched the invasion of Iraq
States as the policy of the United States to pursue regional and international initiatives to assist Iraq in achieving certain security, political, and economic milestones.
Document 5: Reaffirms that the United States is in an armed conflict with the Taliban, al Qaeda, and associated forces
Defines 'unprivileged enemy belligerent' as an individual who has engaged in hostilities against the United States.
Document 6: Requires the Secretary of Defense to submit to the congressional defense committees a plan for the phased redeployment of U.S. Armed Forces from Iraq
Expresses the sense of Congress that the United States should increase its diplomatic efforts within the government of Iraq to help stabilize Iraq and the region.
Document 7: Requires the President to transmit to Congress a national strategy to combat terrorist travel
The President shall direct the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to develop the initial national strategy, an updated strategy, and implementation plans.

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Services
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving the benefits, services, and support for military veterans and their families.

Document 1: Military Retired Pay Fairness Act of 2009
limitations on the recoupment of separation pay
Document 2: space-available travel on military aircraft
members of the reserves who is entitled to retired pay
Document 3: Veterans Mental Health Accessibility Act
eligible for hospital care, medical services, and nursing home care for any mental illness
Document 4: pilot program to assess the feasibility of using service dog training activities
integrated post-deployment mental health and PTSD rehabilitation programs
Document 5: physical examination before the member's separation or discharge
reimbursed for receiving such an examination
Document 6: increases the authorized allowance for burial and funeral expenses
deceased veterans who were in receipt of veterans' disability compensation
Document 7: Veterans' Compensation Equity Act of 2008
assign a disability rating of not less than 10% for veterans requiring continuous medication

topic.name: Healthcare Access and Family Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation focused on improving access to healthcare services while providing support for families navigating caregiving responsibilities.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Labor to make five-year grants to assist families by providing wage replacement for eligible individuals responding to family caregiving needs.
Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993 to allow employees to take specified additional leave for parental involvement and family medical care needs.
Document 2: This bill amends the Indian Health Care Improvement Act by requiring the Indian Health Service to implement a pilot program for testing long-term contracts for rural IHS hospitals with tribal input.
The bill expands the IHS' hiring authority by allowing it to offer more benefits to recruit employees.
Document 3: Amends the Higher Education Act to authorize recruitment incentive grants for improving primary care education and training for medical students.
Establishes an educational loan repayment program for individuals who agree to serve as primary care physicians in areas with a critical shortage.

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Actions and Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative actions and regulations related to labor, environmental management, foreign affairs, and urban development in the United States.

Document 1: Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970
require site-controlling employers to keep a site log for all recordable injuries and illnesses
Document 2: United States Ambassador at Large for Arctic Affairs Act of 2011
establish within the Department of State an Ambassador at Large for Arctic Affairs
Document 3: Safeguard Aquaculture Farmers Act
regulations that allow for the taking of double-crested cormorants
Document 4: Focusing the Fed on the Currency of the United States Act of 2013
repeal the joint mandate on the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
Document 5: appropriations for South Pacific exchanges
educational and cultural exchange programs of the Department of State
Document 6: Prohibits the obligation or expenditure of funds for assistance to Egypt
President certifies to Congress that Egypt is honoring its commitments
Document 7: Revitalizing Cities Through Parks Enhancement Act
grants to qualified community organizations to develop open space municipally owned vacant lots

topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative measures aimed at protecting consumers, enhancing financial security, and ensuring fair practices in financial and personal information management.

Document 1: Whistleblower Augmented Reward and Nonretaliation Act of 2016
protect declarants from employer reprisals
Document 2: Identity Theft Prevention Act
requires any commercial entity to develop a written program for the security of sensitive personal information
Document 3: Stop Outsourcing Banking Enforcement and Examination Act
prescribe requirements with which a federal banking agency must comply
Document 4: TRIA Reform Act of 2014
amends the Terrorism Risk Insurance Act
Document 5: Native American Children's Safety Act
prohibit the final approval of any foster care placement until a criminal records check is completed
Document 6: Consumer Financial Protection Agency Act of 2009
establishes the Consumer Financial Protection Agency as an independent executive agency
Document 7: Internet Poker Freedom Act of 2013
prohibits a person from operating an Internet poker facility without a license

topic.name: Energy Independence Initiatives
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative efforts and programs aimed at achieving energy independence through the development and promotion of alternative energy technologies and efficiency measures.

Document 1: New Manhattan Project for Energy Independence
developing alternative technology vehicles
Document 2: Electric Transportation Advancement Act
qualified electric transportation projects
Document 3: Oil Independence for a Stronger America Act
reduce oil consumption by 8 million barrels per day
Document 4: Solar Energy Regulatory Relief Act
program to provide competitive grants for solar permitting
Document 5: Renewable Fuel Utilization, Expansion, and Leadership Act
renewable and alternative energy infrastructure competitive grant program
Document 6: Americans Making Power Act
require each state regulatory authority to adopt the net metering standard
Document 7: Greening the Healthcare Workforce Act
promote environmentally sustainable practices in building and maintenance operations

topic.name: FDA Regulatory Reforms
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at reforming the regulatory processes of the FDA to enhance the approval and oversight of drugs and medical devices.

Document 1: Compassionate Freedom of Choice Act of 2015
FDA must not prevent or restrict the manufacture, importation, distribution, or sale of investigational drugs or devices for terminally ill patients.
Document 2: Preservation of Antibiotics for Medical Treatment Act of 2007

requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to deny an application for a new animal drug that is a critical antimicrobial animal drug unless the applicant demonstrates that there is a reasonably certainty of no harm to human health.
Document 3: Sensible Oversight for Technology which Advances Regulatory Efficiency Act of 2013
Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FDCA) to apply it to medical software to the same extent and in the same manner as it applies to devices.
Document 5: Advancing Breakthrough Devices for Patients Act of 2016
FDA must determine whether a device meets the criteria for designation as a breakthrough device.
Document 6: amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the FDA to establish a third-party quality system assessment program
Device manufacturers are allowed to make changes to a device without submitting to the FDA the 30-day notice required for manufacturing changes.
Document 7: FDA Accountability for Public Safety Act
requires the Commissioner of Food and Drugs to ensure that an advisory committee of the Center for Drug Evaluation and Research evaluates each new drug application for an opioid.

topic.name: Health Care Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at regulating health care practices, ensuring patient rights, and improving health care access and safety.

Document 1: Pregnant Women Health and Safety Act
Requires a physician who performs an abortion to have admitting privileges at a local hospital.
Document 2: Choice in Health Options Insures Care for Everyone (CHOICE) Act of 2009
Establishes the American Trust Health Plan to offer eligible individuals health benefits coverage.
Document 3: Patients Right to Know Act of 2013
Requires health plans to include in their annual summary of benefits and coverage explanations.
Document 4: Coverage Protection Act
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to require the issuer of the plan to treat such individual as enrolled as of December 23, 2013.
Document 5: Mammogram and MRI Availability Act of 2011
Requires a group or individual health plan that provides coverage for diagnostic mammography to provide no less favorable coverage for annual screening mammography.
Document 6: Nurse and Health Care Worker Protection Act of 2009
Requires health care employers to develop and implement a safe patient handling and injury prevention plan.
Document 7: Good Samaritan Health Professionals Act of 2015
Shields a health care professional from liability under federal or state law for harm caused by any act or omission if serving as a volunteer in response to a disaster.

topic.name: Veterans and Government Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving benefits and support for veterans, as well as accountability in government spending and contracting.

Document 1: Amends the Wounded Warriors Act to extend through 2014 the authority of the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to provide the same rehabilitation and vocational benefits to members of the Armed Forces
Requires (current law encourages) participation by eligible members in the Transitional Assistance Program (TAP) of the Department of Defense (DOD).
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (Secretary) to prescribe regulations specifying the contents of a currently-required notice to claimants of a complete or substantially complete application

Authorizes the U.S. Court of Appeals for Veterans Claims to review actions taken by the Secretary with respect to the adoption and revision of the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) schedule for the rating of veterans' disabilities.
Document 5: Requires the head of each executive agency to establish a policy under which eligible agency employees may be authorized to telework
Directs each agency head to ensure that an interactive telework training program is provided to eligible employees and their managers.

topic.name: Suspension of Tariffs on Goods
topic.description: This cluster discusses temporary suspensions of duties on various imported goods in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

1: suspend temporarily the duty on certain textile device book style covers.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
2: suspend temporarily the duty on certain forged steel crankshafts.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
3: suspend temporarily the duty on certain acrylic fiber tow.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
4: extend the temporary suspension of duty on ethene, tetrafluoro, oxidized, polymerized reduced, methyl esters, reduced, ethoxylated.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
5: suspend temporarily the duty on necklaces or bracelets, other than necklaces or bracelets containing jadeites or rubies, valued $10 each or higher.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
6: suspend temporarily the duty on Steel Ball Bearing, 62 millimeters outside diameter x 30 millimeters inside diameter x 16 millimeters width.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
7: extend the temporary suspension of duty on copper oxychloride and copper hydroxide.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: Public Health Initiatives
topic.description: Legislative acts aimed at improving public health through education, prevention, and research funding.

Document 1: Building a Health Care Workforce for the Future Act
requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award matching grants to enable states to implement scholarship programs
Document 2: Birth Defects Prevention, Risk Reduction, and Awareness Act of 2011
establish and implement a birth defects prevention and public awareness program
Document 3: Tom Lantos Pulmonary Hypertension Research and Education Act of 2011
requires educational campaigns to increase public awareness of pulmonary hypertension
Document 4: Diabetes Prevention Act of 2009
establish a national diabetes prevention program targeted at persons at high risk for diabetes
Document 5: Bone Health Promotion and Research Act of 2009
authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services to develop and implement a National Bone Health Program
Document 6: Hereditary Hemorrhagic Telangiectasia Diagnosis and Treatment Act of 2011
establish and implement a hereditary hemorrhagic telangiectasia initiative
Document 7: National Autism Spectrum Disorders Initiative Act of 2011
establish the National Autism Spectrum Disorders Initiative to improve the lives of persons with autism spectrum disorders

topic.name: Tribal Land and Water Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, conveyance, and rights associated with land and water for various Native American tribes.

Document 1: Water Rights Settlement Act
Directs the Secretary of the Interior to hold tribal water rights in trust.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey to Elko County
Holds 373 acres of BLM administered land in trust for the Te-moak Tribe.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to take into trust two tracts of federal land
Prohibits any gaming activities from being carried out on such land taken into trust.
Document 4: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to convey to the Northern Cheyenne Indian Tribe all its mineral interests
Prohibits the mineral interests underlying the Cheyenne tracts from being subject to taxation.
Document 5: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to take certain land in Yuma County into trust for the Cocopah Indian Tribe
Prohibits using the trust land for gaming under the Indian Gaming Regulatory Act.
Document 6: Amends the Act of June 7, 1956, to extend federal recognition to the Lumbee Tribe
The Department of the Interior may take land into trust for the tribe.
Document 7: Amends the Lower Brule Sioux Tribe Infrastructure Development Trust Fund Act
Provides compensation to the Lower Brule Tribe for damage to tribal land.

topic.name: Election and Voting Regulations
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures aimed at regulating and improving the processes surrounding elections and voting rights in the United States.

Document 1: Authorizes states to delegate responsibilities under UOCAVA
Requires states to establish procedures for absentee uniform services voters and overseas voters
Document 2: Requires the National Labor Relations Board to acquire a list of employees eligible to vote in collective bargaining elections
Document 3: Requires audits of programs funded through earmarks and reporting to Congress
Document 4: Applies a ban on foreign contributions in American elections to domestic corporations with foreign principals
Document 5: Requires federal agencies to review and potentially sunset rules that impact the economy
Document 6: Amends the National Labor Relations Act regarding timelines and procedures for collective bargaining elections
Document 7: Modifies the nomination process for Coast Guard Academy cadets by Congress members

topic.name: Higher Education and Safety Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing safety and accountability in higher education institutions, particularly concerning reporting and addressing campus crimes such as sexual violence.

Document 1: Amends provisions of the Higher Education Act of 1965 known as the Jeanne Clery Disclosure of Campus Security Policy and Campus Crime Statistics Act to require institutions of higher education to include in their annual campus security reports...
Requires IHEs to provide new students and employees with a statement that identifies domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, and stalking as crimes which will be reported...
Document 2: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 (HEA) to revise HEA programs and to extend the authorization of appropriations for them through FY2013.
Revises the general definition of institution of higher education (IHE) to include schools that award degrees...
Document 5: Directs each military department Secretary to implement a program providing a Special Victims' Counsel to a victim of a sexual assault committed by a member of the Armed Forces...
Requires the Director to also collect, maintain, and disseminate...

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Legislative Reforms
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts aimed at reforming and enhancing Medicare and Medicaid services and benefits for beneficiaries.

Document 1: establish a demonstration program for the provision of integrated care to Medicare beneficiaries with end-stage renal disease
return savings achieved under the models to program-eligible beneficiaries
Document 2: apply the Medicaid primary care payment rate in 2013 and 2014 to additional physicians
Document 3: extend to nurse practitioners, physician assistants, clinical nurse specialists, and certified nurse midwives payment for primary care services
Document 4: direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to study whether there should be an increase in the prospective payment rate for inpatient rehabilitation services
Document 5: repeal the prohibition against interference by the Secretary of Health and Human Services with the negotiations between drug manufacturers and pharmacies
grant the Secretary authority to negotiate contracts with manufacturers of covered part D drugs
Document 6: provide access to services under medication therapy management programs for Medicare part D eligible individuals
application of this Act only if the Chief Actuary determines that such application... is not projected to increase overall costs
Document 7: permit physical therapy to be furnished under the Medicare program to individuals under the care of a dentist

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Consumer Protection
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at regulating financial institutions and protecting consumers in various financial transactions.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to prescribe requirements for deposit restricted qualified tuition programs which are exempt from federal income tax.
Defines such a program as one in which the cash provided to it by a contributor may be invested only in deposits insured by the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC).
Document 2: Amends the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 to revise the authority of the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Declares unlawful for a broker, dealer, funding portal, or municipal securities dealer to enter into, modify, or extend an agreement with customers or clients governing a future dispute.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to prohibit an insured depository institution from being or becoming an affiliate of any insurance company, securities entity, or swaps entity.
Makes certain technical and conforming amendments to the Banking Act of 1933 and the Revised Statutes of the United States to limit the business of national banks.
Document 4: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to deem a mortgage broker, in the case of a home mortgage loan, to have a fiduciary relationship with the consumer.
Requires each mortgage originator to act with respect to each home mortgage loan with reasonable skill, care, and diligence.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Credit Union Act regarding insured amounts payable in connection with a bankrupt state-chartered credit union.
Defines 'IOLTA' as a system in which lawyers place certain client funds in interest-bearing or dividend-bearing accounts.
Document 6: Amends the Consumer Credit Protection Act to prescribe consumer protection guidelines for rental-purchase transactions.
Grants enforcement powers to the Federal Trade Commission and to state attorneys general.
Document 7: Prohibits the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve from taking any action to implement any rule relating to credit cards published in the Federal Register.
Amends the Truth in Lending Act to direct the Board to issue regulations with respect to credit card accounts.

topic.name: Transportation Safety and Security
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing safety and security standards across various modes of transportation, including maritime, aviation, rail, and mining.

Document 1: Amends port security provisions to direct the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating to establish an international committee for the safe and secure handling and transportation of especially hazardous cargo.
Establishes regional response and recovery protocols to prepare for, respond to, mitigate against, and recover from a transportation security incident.
Document 2: Reauthorizes appropriations for FAA operations and airport planning and development to enhance aviation safety.
Directs the FAA Administrator to establish user fees for certain FAA services, contributing to the financial sustainability of safety programs.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Labor to determine why an accident occurred in coal or other mines and make recommendations to avoid any recurrence.
Mandates independent accident investigations for severe mining accidents to enhance safety accountability.
Document 4: Establishes an independent National Mine Safety Board to conduct investigations of mine accidents, promoting transparency and accountability in mine safety.
Requires mine inspections to be conducted during various shifts and days of the week when miners are normally present.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Commerce and the Coast Guard to enforce laws concerning fisheries and fish products, ensuring environmental and safety compliance in fisheries management.
Document 6: Authorizes appropriations for FAA operations and civil aviation research to improve air transportation safety.
Requires the FAA Administrator to report on airport passenger enplanements, contributing to safety assessments.
Document 7: Reauthorizes appropriations for rail safety activities for the safe transportation of hazardous materials.
Prescribes requirements for the operation of high-hazard flammable trains to enhance rail safety.

topic.name: Housing Assistance and Community Development
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative efforts aimed at improving housing conditions, providing assistance to vulnerable populations, and promoting community regeneration.

Document 1: Amends the Residential Lead-Based Paint Hazard Reduction Act of 1992 to revise the purpose for grants for lead-based paint hazard reduction in target housing.
Requires such grants to be made instead for reduction of lead-based paint hazards and correction of other housing-related hazards.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to carry out a demonstration program to encourage and test innovative vacant property reclamation and urban infrastructure renewal strategies.
Limits such agreements to local governments experiencing at least 15% population loss since 1970.
Document 4: Amends the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act to authorize the President to increase the federal share of financial assistance for individuals and households affected by disasters.
Requires FEMA to award a grant for the full amount that Congress makes available for the Emergency Food and Shelter Program.

topic.name: Tax Policy Amendments
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at modifying tax laws and regulations.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, as amended by the American Taxpayer Relief Act of 2012
Amends the Regulated Investment Company Modernization Act of 2010
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a qualified small business to elect to use a portion of its tax credit for increasing research expenditures
Defines 'qualified small business' as a corporation, a partnership, or a person other than a tax-exempt organization
Document 3: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to impose a consumption tax
It also lowers individual and corporate income tax rates
Document 4: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to authorize the designation of opportunity zones
provides tax incentives for investments in the zones
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to estate, gift, and generation-skipping transfer taxes
revise estate tax rates to impose an increased tax for estates over $3.5 million
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the reduction in the marriage penalty applicable to the earned income tax credit
increase such tax credit for families with three or more qualifying children
Document 7: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to prevent qualified business income attributed to individuals from being taxed at a higher rate than corporate income
Qualified business income is all items of income, deduction, loss, or credit properly attributable to the taxpayer from the active conduct of a trade or business

topic.name: Corporate Tax Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code that impact corporate taxation and related deductions.

Document 1: amend the Internal Revenue Code to revise the income tax rates on corporations
impose a single 20% rate on corporate taxable income
Document 2: amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the classification of motorsports entertainment complex
for depreciation purposes as seven-year property
Document 3: amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend the period in which a corporation may carryover excess contributions
for purposes of the tax deduction for charitable contributions from 5 to 20 succeeding taxable years
Document 4: amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the special depreciation allowance
for certain property acquired after December 31, 2007
Document 5: amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase the bonus depreciation for new business equipment
placed in service in 2008 and 2009
Document 6: amends the Internal Revenue Code to reduce the rate of the tentative alternative minimum tax
for noncorporate taxpayers
Document 7: amends the Internal Revenue Code with respect to the home office tax deduction
to allow the use of a portion of a dwelling unit used for conducting a trade or business for the care of a dependent child

topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at protecting consumers in different sectors, including finance, pharmaceuticals, and product safety.

Document 1: Credit Card Accountability Responsibility and Disclosure Act of 2009
Amends the Truth in Lending Act (TILA), with respect to credit card accounts under an open end consumer credit plan.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to establish requirements to facilitate the tracing of drug products through the pharmaceutical supply distribution chain.
Provides requirements of standards for the exchange of transaction documentation.
Document 3: Amends the Trade Act of 1974 to allow the filing of a petition for certification of eligibility to apply for trade adjustment assistance by adversely affected workers in a service sector firm.
Authorizes the Secretary of Commerce to provide technical assistance to service industries adversely affected by import competition.
Document 4: Treats as a banned hazardous substance under the Federal Hazardous Substances Act (FHSA) any children's product containing more than specified amounts of lead.
Requires periodic review and, when technologically feasible, more stringent limits.
Document 5: Amends the Jenkins Act to revise provisions governing the collection of taxes on, and trafficking in, cigarettes and smokeless tobacco.
Requires sellers of tobacco products to file with the Attorney General reports filed with state tobacco tax administrators.
Document 6: Expresses the sense of Congress that economic sanctions, diplomacy, and military planning are necessary to compel Iran to abandon efforts to acquire a nuclear weapons capability.
Urges the President to initiate diplomatic efforts to expand the multilateral sanctions regime regarding Iran.
Document 7: Amends the Workforce Investment Act of 1998 to direct the Secretary of Labor to establish a Manufacturing Job Training National Program.
Directs the Secretary to provide workplace development and economic assistance to eligible recipients affected by the base realignment and closure (BRAC) activities.

topic.name: Legislative Measures and Designations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures related to the designation of postal facilities and amendments to judicial and public building regulations.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 3031 Veterans Road West in Staten Island, New York, as the 'Leonard Montalto Post Office Building.'
Document 2: This bill amends the federal judicial code to convert certain temporary bankruptcy judges to permanent bankruptcy judges.
Document 3: Requires a restroom in a public building to be a family restroom or otherwise to be equipped with baby changing facilities.
Document 4: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 1900 West Gray Street in Houston, Texas, as the 'Hazel Hainsworth Young Post Office Building.'
Document 5: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 95 Dogwood Street in Cary, Mississippi, as the 'Spencer Byrd Powers Jr. Post Office.'
Document 6: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 6691 Church Street in Riverdale, Georgia, as the 'Major Gregory E. Barney Post Office Building.'
Document 7: This bill authorizes the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission to extend by six years the time period during which Gibson Dam Hydroelectric Company is required to commence construction.

topic.name: Transportation and Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on transportation infrastructure, animal conservation, and welfare standards in the context of federal policies.

Document 1: annual audit of the financial statements of the Union Station Redevelopment Corporation
Document 2: 25% of amounts appropriated to the Secretary of Transportation for capital grants to states for rail line relocation and improvement projects
Document 3: Restoration of the U.S.-Russia Polar Bear Conservation Fund Act of 2013
Document 4: Animal Welfare in Agricultural Research Endeavors Act or the AWARE Act
Document 5: Amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to prohibit treatment of the Gray Wolf as an endangered or threatened species
Document 6: excludes appraisers of employee stock ownership plans from the meaning of a fiduciary subject to ERISA
Document 7: makes federal government officials responsible for innovative technology loan guarantees personally liable for violations
